reason: Response A shows more sophisticated, integrated prose with subtler observations (the metronome contrast, "occupies just enough of the auditory field," the "narrative quality" of being sheltered) and flowing sentences that build a unified argument, hallmarks of the more capable Sonnet tier. Response B is competent but more formulaic and list-like, organizing thoughts into discrete buckets (acoustic properties, then context) with more conventional phrasing, which fits the smaller Haiku model. The greater conceptual depth and stylistic cohesion of A point to Sonnet.
